lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Time to play! These games make our moves quick and strong.

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Bet you can't beat them all. I'll be watching… and giggling.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: We'll show Murk what we can do. Ready, Caller?

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: We cleared the playground! Our moves are quick now.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: But up ahead I see a big grey gate, and Murk is sitting on it…

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Stop right there! No one crosses my gate without passing my riddles.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: We've learned so much, Caller. Answer Murk's questions and the gate will open!

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Riddles about squares, roads, and pieces. Get them right… if you can!

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Gah! You actually passed? Fine, fine — the gate is open.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Look, Caller! The whole Square Kingdom has its colors back!

lines.speaker: rookwell
lines.text: …Who woke me? I am Sir Rookwell. Beyond this gate, in Battle Meadow, I will teach you to be brave.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Our first friend! Come on — Battle Meadow is waiting!

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: None shall pass the cliff gate without proving they can win AND stay safe!

lines.speaker: belle
lines.text: You are ready, Caller. Answer Murk, and the heights are ours.

lines.speaker: belle
lines.text: The mountains shine again! Thank you, Caller — I am wide awake.

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Two friends woken… this is getting out of hand!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Down to Champions' Road, Caller. I hear something fast and bouncy down there…

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: My trickiest riddles yet! Forks, pins, pawns — get them ALL right, or turn back!

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: We've got this, Caller. Show Murk how sharp you are!

lines.speaker: aurora
lines.text: Light… at last. I am Queen Aurora. You have woken almost the whole kingdom, brave Caller.

lines.speaker: aurora
lines.text: Only the King still sleeps. To wake him, Pip must cross to the far side and become a queen.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: The far side… my dream! Come on, team — to Pip's Arena!

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: Boing! Boing! Hi-hi-hi! I'm Nim the knight — fastest hopper on Champions' Road!

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: Wanna win? START strong! Grab the middle, wake your pieces up, keep your king cozy.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Good first moves! Show us, Caller — let's open like champions.

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: Ooh, you're GOOD. Can I come with you? I wanna help wake everyone too!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Of course, Nim! Now we're a team of three — me, Nim, and you, Caller!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Oh… where are all the colors? The whole kingdom turned grey!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Everyone is fast asleep. The Grey put them to sleep — even the King!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: I'm Pip, the smallest pawn. And YOU are my Caller. Together we can bring the colors back!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: First I must learn this place. Help me explore the board of 64 squares!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Look! When we learned the squares, a tiny bit of color came back!

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Hee-hee! A pawn playing hero? You'll never wake them all, little Pip.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: That's Murk, the trickster magpie. Don't worry — we'll prove him wrong, Caller!

lines.speaker: aurora
lines.text: Here sleeps King Cedric, where the Grey is strongest. Only real games will break it.

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: This is your LAST step, Pip. Play me, level by level… I won't make it easy!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Everything we learned comes down to this. Play with me, Caller — all the way to the crown!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: I reached the far side… I'm a QUEEN! The crown is mine!

lines.speaker: cedric
lines.text: Ho! The Grey is lifted and I am awake. Thank you, Caller — you saved the Kingdom of Sixty-Four!

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: I… I only made trouble because nobody ever played with me. Could… could I stay?

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Of course, Murk. Every kingdom needs a trickster friend. We did it, Caller — together!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: To wake the sleeping pieces, we must travel the kingdom's roads.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Some roads go straight — up, down, and across. Some go slanted, like a diagonal.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Learn the roads with me, and we can reach anyone who needs us!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Now I can see the roads glowing! Straight ones and slanted ones.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: More color is coming back. The kingdom is waking up, bit by bit!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Here are three sleepy friends: the King, the Knight, and another little pawn like me.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Each one moves in its own special way. If we learn how they move, we can wake them!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Did you see? They wiggled! Learning their moves is waking them up.

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Pfft. Knowing the moves and USING them are very different, hero.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Then let's practice, Caller — to the playground!

lines.speaker: rookwell
lines.text: Welcome to Battle Meadow! Here, pieces can bump enemies off the board. That's a capture!

lines.speaker: rookwell
lines.text: But some pieces are worth more than others. A wise fighter knows what to trade.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Teach us, Sir Rookwell! Help us learn what each piece is worth, Caller.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: The grass is turning green! We know what the pieces are worth now.

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Worth, schmorth. You'll give away your queen the moment you stop looking!

lines.speaker: rookwell
lines.text: When the king is attacked, that's CHECK — he must escape at once.

lines.speaker: rookwell
lines.text: When he's attacked and CAN'T escape — that's checkmate. That's how chess is won!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Show the Grey what we've learned, Caller. Find the checkmate!

lines.speaker: rookwell
lines.text: Checkmate! You did it. Battle Meadow is green and golden once more.

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Hmph. Beginner's luck. The high cliffs of Castle Heights will stop you!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Up we climb, Caller — someone is sleeping on the heights, and we'll wake her too!

lines.speaker: belle
lines.text: Mmm… who climbs so high? I am Bishop Belle. Up here we keep the kingdom's SECRET moves.

lines.speaker: belle
lines.text: Castling, en passant, and a pawn becoming a queen — special moves that surprise your foe.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Secret moves?! Teach us every one, Belle!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Castling AND turning a pawn into a queen? These are wonderful!

lines.speaker: belle
lines.text: You learn quickly, Caller. But there is one more thing every player must know…

lines.speaker: belle
lines.text: Not every game is won, little one. Sometimes a game ends in a tie — a draw.

lines.speaker: belle
lines.text: Beware STALEMATE: if a king can't move but isn't in check, the game is a draw — not a win!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: So I must be careful NOT to stalemate when I'm winning. Got it!

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Clever, clever. But my gate on the heights is trickier than the last…

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: Two rooks, a queen, a single rook — each one can checkmate a lonely king. Let's learn the patterns!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Walk the king to the edge, then snap the trap shut. You can do it, Caller!

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: Boom! Mate! The whole road is sparkling again. Three friends awake!

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: No, no, NO! Stay out of my Trickster Forest — it's full of nasty surprises!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Surprises don't scare us anymore. Into the forest, team!

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: My forest! Here I hide my favorite tricks — forks, pins, and skewers. You'll never learn them!

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: Oh-ho, a FORK hits two things at once! We can use Murk's own tricks against him!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Let's master every trick, Caller — and find who's sleeping deep in these trees.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Forks, pins, skewers — they're OURS now!

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Hey! Those are MY tricks! …this isn't going how I planned.

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: Ready for my favorites, Caller? Mates where the king is trapped by its OWN pieces!

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: The back-rank mate and the smothered mate — sneaky and beautiful. Let's spring them!

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: Back-rank AND smothered — you've got a whole bag of checkmates now!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Next, Caller — the great king-and-queen and king-and-rook mates. Let's finish the road!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: This part is close to my heart, Caller. A pawn who marches all the way home becomes a QUEEN.

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: That's YOUR dream, Pip! Let's practice walking pawns home, step by step.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Every pawn made it home! My own dream feels closer than ever.

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: One last gate, deep in the trees. Pass it… if you dare.

lines.speaker: rookwell
lines.text: A brave piece is also a CAREFUL one. Before every move, look: is anything in danger?

lines.speaker: rookwell
lines.text: Murk loves it when you forget to check. Let's not give him the chance.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: I looked before I leaped — and nothing got taken for free!

lines.speaker: rookwell
lines.text: Well done, Caller. Now for the most important move of all… the checkmate.

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: Psst, Caller — some tricksters try to win in just FOUR moves! It's called Scholar's Mate.

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: They gang up on one square — f7. Learn to see it, and nobody can ever catch you with it!

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: Boing! You spotted it AND turned it around. Four-move traps don't scare us!

lines.speaker: murk
lines.text: Bah — I was SAVING that trick. No fair learning it!

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: To catch a king, build a BOX! Take away his squares one by one until he's stuck.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Like herding a sheep into a pen. Let's practice the trap, Caller!

lines.speaker: nim
lines.text: Boxed him in! You're a natural king-catcher, Caller!

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Now let's turn those traps into real checkmates.
